headline: Bernard Arnault's business-first strategy with foreign heads of state, from Trump to Putin
subhead: 'Bernard Arnault's Empire' (3/6). The head of LVMH, which generates 70% of its revenue outside Europe, has fostered relationships with the leaders of the markets he targets. Foremost among them is the president of the United States, whom he has courted since the 1980s.

headline: French MPs pass law banning social media for under-15s
Why 19 men have been charged in a French mass rapes case
Zelensky sacks army chief Syrsky after days of protests
subhead: Volodymyr Zelensky announced he was appointing joint forces commander Mykhailo Drapaty in his place. Questions over Oleksandr Syrsky's future emerged last week, when Zelensky removed highly popular defense minister Mykhailo Fedorov just six months after appointing him.

headline: 'Stopping AI because it carries risks? That would be like banning cars because they cause accidents'
subhead: The challenge for humans is not choosing between accelerating or slowing down artificial intelligence, but creating the conditions for public trust, argue entrepreneur Eric Hazan and economist Olivier Sibony for Le Monde.

headline: Sleeping Beauty syndrome: 'For a week, I slept 15 to 20 hours out of every 24'
subhead: This extremely rare condition, also known as Kleine-Levin syndrome (KLS), mainly affects boys. It is characterized by episodes of massive hypersomnia lasting one or more weeks, often accompanied by cognitive and sometimes behavioral disturbances.

headline: Copenhagen emerges as new pop laboratory
subhead: A new Danish scene, made up mostly of women, has been offering an alternative, minimalist and sensual pop for several years. Many of the musicians, who often trained at the city's conservatory, have taken their music beyond the borders of Scandinavia.

subhead: Le Monde's critics present our selection of exhibitions on painting, contemporary art and photography across France.

headline: Didier Deschamps bids farewell to French team after spectacular World Cup defeat
subhead: The coach stepped down after a dramatic 6-4 loss in the World Cup third-place match against England on Saturday. Over his 14 years in the role, he nonetheless managed to restore France to the pinnacle of world football.

headline: In Paris café, terror attack survivors find community: 'People want to tell each other: I am alive'
subhead: After long days attending the 2015 Paris attacks trial, survivors and relatives of victims met in a bar to comfort one another. They have continued to gather every month, joined by victims of other attacks, to share experiences, advice and, above all, laughter and good company.
